content: I've created a public google calendar and notified everybody on #seminars and events, proposing what I said before that everybody re-post the same link whenever they create a new event
It turns out that you need a google account to view even a public google calendar
If anyone knows a decent open-source calendar alternative, now would be a good time to suggest it before we get too heavily invested in the google calendar
Update: It might be that you don't need a google account and the settings just hadn't switched yet, still waiting to be sure
The Google calendar is now at https://categorytheory.world/
thanks @Daniel Geisler: that looks great!

Here are the calendars I have loaded in the Google calendar. I can also look at doing this in TeamUp if folks like it better.
Category Theory Virtual Seminars
categorytheoryseminars@gmail.com
HoTT Electronic Seminar Talks
https://ucr.zoom.us/meeting/u5Qqdu-oqDsrnNuE0v8386uhHBWr4GQpqA/ics?icsToken=98tyKu-oqTosGtKVsVyCY7ctA8Hib9_ykH9gv4YNike2W3ZGaivUAfAWFYNvAfmB
MIT Categories Seminar
OWLS

Thanks for doing these. However, I think we do want to use TeamUp, mainly because it is easier to create different calendars and designate editors for them. Could you import this, when you get a chance? https://ics.teamup.com/feed/kswejyg8r137xttzjt/0.ics
